leadText: Heatran (Japanese: ヒードラン Heatran) is a dual-type Fire/Steel Legendary Pokémon introduced in Generation IV. While it is not known to evolve into or from any other Pokémon, Heatran can Mega Evolve into Mega Heatran using the Heatranite. Mega Heatran was introduced in the Mega Dimension DLC for Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Heatran climbing on the ceiling of a cave Heatran is a reddish-brown, quadrupedal Pokémon . It is dotted with orange and gray spots, and it has metal bangles locked on its legs. Its four claws on each of its feet are in a cross formation. Heatran has a gray underside, and its face is covered by an iron mask, with red-orange eyes uncovered. Its eyes seem to remain open even when it is asleep. Whenever it is seen with its mouth open, an orange glow emanates from inside it. Boiling, magma-like blood circulates through its body. While its body is rugged steel, certain parts of it are partially melted, due to Heatran's own intense body heat. Heatran is believed to the embodiment of the planet's boiling core. According to legends, Heatran is said to have been birthed from the boiling magma within volcanic mountains such as Mount Coronet and Reversal Mountain . A Magma Stone is capable of both controlling and sealing Heatran within its lair. Without the power of the stone, it would rampage and cause the volcanic crater where it dwells to erupt. Heatran's claws allow it to dig deep into walls. Its feet allow it to stick to surfaces so it can hang and climb on walls and ceilings. The body of Heatran is said to hold many unsolved mysteries. Heatran has the ability to form a shield around itself in order to protect itself from harm. Heatran is the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Magma Storm . Forms [ edit | edit source ] Heatran can Mega Evolve into Mega Heatran. As a result of Mega Evolution, Mega Heatran is much bigger and heavier, and its body temperature increases to extreme degrees. Overall, the reddish-brown on Heatran's body has turned into a more vivid reddish-orange. Mega Heatran's eyes are yellow, and its lower jaw seems to have partially melted or weakened from the more intense heat that it now generates, leaving its mouth hanging open. Inside of its mouth, only a bright yellow and orange glow is visible. The horns on its head have blobs dropping down. Its underside has grown in volume and turned dark red, with its magma-like blood visible from the folds, and its hind legs have completely melted into liquid metal. Its forelimbs are bulkier and kept more intact, though their layers melt over each other, acting as the sole anchor points for Mega Heatran's locomotion. Mega Heatran releases volcanic gases from its back; slightly closer to its head, two gray metal nubs poke out. Mega Heatran can unleash extreme heat to keep enemies at bay, reaching temperatures over 1.8 million °F (approximately 1,000,000 °C) when it goes all-out. Mega Heatran

sections.text: Contents 1 Game data 1.1 NPC appearances 1.1.1 In the core series 1.1.2 In the side series games 1.1.3 In the spin-off games 1.2 Pokédex entries 1.3 Game locations 1.3.1 In side games 1.3.2 In events 1.3.2.1 In-game events 1.4 Stats 1.4.1 Base stats 1.4.1.1 Heatran 1.4.1.2 Mega Heatran 1.4.2 Pokéathlon stats 1.5 Type effectiveness 1.6 Learnset 1.6.1 By leveling up 1.6.2 By TM 1.6.3 By breeding 1.7 Side game data 1.8 Form data 1.8.1 Mega Evolution 1.9 Evolution data 1.10 Sprites Game data [ edit | edit source ] NPC appearances [ edit | edit source ] In the core series [ edit | edit source ] These notable Trainers all use Heatran: In all Generation IV games, as well as Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl , in the Battle Tower , Palmer will use a Heatran during the 49th consecutive battle in Diamond, Pearl , Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, or the Gold Print challenge in Platinum , HeartGold and SoulSilver . Additionally, in Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, Palmer may use a Heatran in Master Class battles, including during a Double Battle with Barry against the player. In Pokémon Platinum , HeartGold and SoulSilver , during a Multi Battle challenge in the Battle Tower , one of the Pokémon that Mira can select to help the player is a Heatran. In Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, one of the Pokémon that Byron may use is a Heatran. Additionally, it may also appear on his team during a Double Battle with Roark against the player. Additionally in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, one of the Pokémon that Candice may use is a Heatran. A Rogue Mega-Evolved Heatran is a boss during the events of Pokémon Legends: Z-A 's DLC, Mega Dimension . After being defeated and caught by the player, it will offer the Heatranite . In the side series games [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Battle Revolution , during the Masters Battle Set 7 , Kruger will use a Heatran as part of his team. In the spin-off games [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Mystery Dungeon: Explorers of Time and Explorers of Darkness : Heatran is the guardian of the Fiery Drum . Pokémon Ranger: Shadows of Almia : Heatran is the guardian of the Red Gem . Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation IV. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry DP169 Heatran Dawn's Pokédex Heatran, the Lava Dome Pokémon. Heatran lives in volcanic caves, and uses its cross-shaped claws to maneuver across stone walls.
